channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Trump Warns Iran "Clock Is Ticking" as Peace Talks Stall
item.description: President Trump issued a stern warning to Iran as peace negotiations show signs of stalling, with Iranian media reporting the US has not made concrete concessions in response to Tehran's latest proposals.

item.title: Israel Built Secret Military Bases in Iraq Before Iran War
item.description: Israel spent over a year preparing covert military sites in western Iraq before launching operations against Iran, according to New York Times reporting. Iraqi officials later confirmed the existence of a second base.

item.title: Ukraine Launches Large-Scale Drone Strike on Moscow Region
item.description: Ukraine conducted a major drone attack on the Moscow region, killing at least three people. President Zelensky said the strikes were a "justified" response to deadly Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.

item.title: Taiwan President Defends Sovereignty After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: President Lai Ching-te stated Taiwan will not provoke conflict nor give up sovereignty, responding to discussions about Taiwan independence during the Trump-Xi meeting in Beijing.

item.title: China to Buy Billions in US Agricultural Products After Trump-Xi Talks
item.description: China agreed to purchase at least $17 billion annually in US agricultural products including beef and poultry following the Trump-Xi summit, marking a trade breakthrough amid broader tensions.

item.title: Israeli Navy Intercepts Gaza-Bound Global Sumud Flotilla
item.description: Israel's navy intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters, preventing activists from reaching the besieged territory.

item.title: Lukashenko Moves Troops Near Ukraine Border
item.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko has moved troops toward the Ukrainian border, raising concerns about a potential new front in the ongoing conflict.

item.title: Witkoff Discusses Ukraine Peace Plan with Putin
item.description: US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff held talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin about a potential peace framework for Ukraine, as diplomatic efforts continue.

item.title: AP Reports on Gaza Journalist Access Restrictions
item.description: The Associated Press examines restrictions on journalist access to Gaza, raising concerns about coverage of the war zone and information flow.

item.title: Australian Workers Strike at INPEX LNG Facility
item.description: Australian union workers announced a strike at INPEX's LNG facility scheduled for May 27, potentially affecting global gas supplies.

item.title: Chinese Scientists Land on Sandy Cay Reef as Philippines Tensions Escalate
item.description: Chinese scientists landed on Sandy Cay reef in the South China Sea amid renewed tensions with the Philippines over disputed territorial claims.

item.title: Austrian Jets Intercept US Military Planes for Second Consecutive Day
item.description: Austrian fighter jets intercepted US military aircraft for the second day in a row, highlighting tensions over airspace rights and military protocols.

item.title: Israel's F-35s Getting External Fuel Tanks
item.description: Israel is upgrading its F-35 fleet with external fuel tanks, extending the range of its stealth fighters for potential long-range operations.

item.title: Middle East Oil Crisis Cost Global Businesses $25 Billion
item.description: The ongoing Middle East oil crisis has already cost global businesses an estimated $25 billion, with further economic impacts expected.

item.title: Global Bond Rout Deepens as Inflation Fears Mount
item.description: Global bond markets experienced a significant sell-off as investors grapple with persistent inflation concerns and central bank policy uncertainty.

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, with around 246 cases and 80 deaths.

item.title: Earthquake Hits Southwest China, Thousands Evacuate Buildings
item.description: An earthquake struck southwestern China, causing buildings to collapse and prompting mass evacuations. Authorities are assessing the damage.

item.title: Cuba Seeking Russian Drones and Military Equipment, US Intelligence Reveals
item.description: US intelligence reports indicate Cuba is seeking to acquire Russian drones and military equipment, potentially deepening military ties between Havana and Moscow.

item.title: Turkish Arms Subsidiary Rebrands After Israel Sales Backlash
item.description: A Turkish arms subsidiary of Repkon has rebranded following backlash over sales to Israel during the Iran war, highlighting domestic political pressures.

item.title: Renewed Tigray Tensions Raise Fears of Ethiopia-Eritrea War
item.description: Fresh tensions in Ethiopia's Tigray region have raised concerns about a potential renewed conflict between Ethiopia and Eritrea.

item.title: Zelensky Touts Shift in Balance After Moscow Attack, Signals More Deep Strikes
item.description: Following an attack on Moscow, President Zelensky highlighted a shift in the military balance and signaled continued deep strikes into Russian territory.

item.title: Hungary Election 2026: Key Questions as Orban Faces Challenge
item.description: Hungary's upcoming election pits Viktor Orban's Fidesz party against Peter Magyar's Tisza party in a crucial contest for the country's political future.

item.title: Climate Change Reducing Oxygen in Rivers Worldwide
item.description: Scientists have found that climate change is reducing oxygen levels in rivers globally, creating "dead zones" that threaten aquatic ecosystems.

item.title: China's Economy Slows Sharply in April, Output and Retail Sales Miss Forecasts
item.description: China's economic growth slowed in April as industrial output and retail sales significantly undershot forecasts, raising concerns about the world's second-largest economy.

item.title: Oil Rises After Drone Attack on UAE Nuclear Power Plant
item.description: Oil prices rose more than 1% following a drone attack on a UAE nuclear power plant, raising concerns about regional energy infrastructure security.
